content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 10,
1992
John Gregg
1000 Peppermill Court
Concord,
CA
94518—1746
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-92-148
Dear Mr.
Gregg:
This is in reply to your letter requesting advice concerning
the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)
As an employee of the Contra Costa Water District you must
complete a Statement of Economic Interests
(Form 730).
You have
asked if you must disclose your community property interest in
your spouse’s salary if your spouse is employed by a local
governmental agency.
Salary and reimbursement for expenses from a local, state,
or
federal government agency are excluded from the Act’s definition
of “income.”
Therefore,
you are not required to disclose your
spouse’s income from the Central Contra Costa Sanitary District on
Schedule D-Income.
If I can be of further assistance, please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
0
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9S804-0807
0
(916) 322-S660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether, as an employee of the Contra Costa Water District required to file a Statement of Economic Interests (Form 730), he must disclose his community property interest in his spouse's salary earned from the Central Contra Costa Sanitary District.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Salary and reimbursements from government agencies are excluded from the definition of "income" under the Political Reform Act, so the requester is not required to disclose his spouse's government salary on Schedule D-Income.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must you disclose your community property interest in your spouse's salary if your spouse is employed by a local governmental agency?

CONCLUSION: Salary and reimbursement for expenses from a local, state, or federal government agency are excluded from the Act's definition of "income." Therefore, you are not required to disclose your spouse's income from the Central Contra Costa Sanitary District on Schedule D-Income.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission April 10, 1992 John Gregg 1000 Peppermill Court Concord, CA 94518—1746 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-92-148 Dear Mr. Gregg: This is in reply to your letter requesting advice concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) As an employee of the Contra Costa Water District you must complete a Statement of Economic Interests (Form 730). You have asked if you must disclose your community property interest in your spouse’s salary if your spouse is employed by a local governmental agency. Salary and reimbursement for expenses from a local, state, or federal government agency are excluded from the Act’s definition of “income.” Therefore, you are not required to disclose your spouse’s income from the Central Contra Costa Sanitary District on Schedule D-Income. If I can be of further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Colleen McGee Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 0 P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9S804-0807 0 (916) 322-S660
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that an employee of Contra Costa Water District is not required to disclose his spouse's salary from the Central Contra Costa Sanitary District because government salaries are excluded from the Act's definition of "income."
